topic: Curated factual technical knowledge compiled by the community

entries.q: What is the purpose of the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge repository is a collection of various materials and tools used daily in work, containing useful information gathered in one piece.

entries.q: Who is the Book of Secret Knowledge repository aimed at?
entries.a: The repository is aimed at System and Network administrators, DevOps, Pentesters, and Security Researchers, but everyone can find useful information.

entries.q: What type of license does the Book of Secret Knowledge repository use?
entries.a: The repository uses the MIT License.

entries.q: How can users contribute to the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Users can contribute by making pull requests, adding valid and well-reasoned explanations about their changes or comments, and following the contributing guidelines.

entries.q: What types of tools and resources are included in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: The repository includes CLI tools, GUI tools, web tools, systems and services, networks, containers and orchestration, manuals, howtos, tutorials, inspiring lists, blogs, podcasts, videos, hacking and penetration testing resources, and more.

entries.q: How can users stay updated with changes and new additions to the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Users can use the RSS/Atom feed of the commits to stay informed about all changes, and new items are added on a regular basis.

entries.q: What are some examples of CLI tools included in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Examples of CLI tools include GNU Bash, Zsh, tclsh, bash-it, Oh My ZSH!, Oh My Fish, Starship, and powerlevel10k.

entries.q: What are some examples of shell plugins included in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Examples of shell plugins include z, fzf, zsh-autosuggestions, zsh-syntax-highlighting, and Awesome ZSH Plugins.
